design_philosophy.core_loop: Face a variety of opponents in best-of-three matches using martial arts combos and special moves
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Execute learned fighting patterns and special moves (Desperation Moves) against diverse opponent movesets while managing health across multiple rounds
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Martial artist (Ryo Sakazaki or Robert Garcia) mastering Kyokugenryu Karate to rescue kidnapped allies and defeat criminal organizations
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Series emphasizes fighting engine and storytelling as core pillars; uses taunt mechanics strategically as part of combat design; integrates narrative progression through character story modes rather than standalone arcade presentation

progression.upgrade_system: Special moves and Desperation Moves obtained as progression rewards in story mode; integrated into core moveset by sequels
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story progression unlocks new characters and special techniques; arcade tournament structure progresses through opponent ladder
replayability_hooks: Two-player versus mode with eight of ten fighters playable by default
Multiple character story modes (Ryo and Robert as primary story characters)
Character roster expansion in sequels with new playable fighters
Cheat code unlocks for hidden characters (Mr. Big, Mr. Karate)

world_building_techniques: Story mode structure with character-specific narratives
Plot progression through tournament brackets (Art of Fighting 2)
Character arcs connected to franchise universe (crossovers with Fatal Fury, leading to King of Fighters)
Cliffhanger narrative design (first game ends with reveal pending about Mr. Karate's identity)
Multi-character perspective (Art of Fighting 2 expands beyond Ryo/Robert focus; Art of Fighting 3 shifts to Robert Garcia's story)

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters function as narrative climax points; Mr. Karate serves as mysterious final boss with identity tied to story revelation; progression toward increasingly powerful opponents mirrors escalation of conflict across trilogy
notable_bosses: Mr. Karate (enigmatic final boss, reveals family connection)
Geese Howard (Art of Fighting 2, mafia antagonist)

faction_indicators: Criminal organizations (Mr. Big's syndicate, mafia forces under Geese Howard)
Martial arts tradition (Kyokugenryu Karate lineage and students)
danger_signals: Kidnapping plot driving narrative (Yuri abduction as inciting incident)
Mafia manipulation and control of tournament
Antagonist pursuit of powerful supernatural elixir (Art of Fighting 3)
environmental_storytelling: Glasshill Valley, Mexico setting (Art of Fighting 3) establishes Robert's personal journey geography
Tournament structure implies controlled fighting venues and organized competition
Character movesets and special techniques reflect training in distinct martial traditions
